hooks.prompt: Check .claude/nerd.local.md for the research backlog. If it has 'proposed' entries, mention: 'Nerd: {N} proposals in backlog. Run /nerd to start.' Also check for stale 'running' entries — if any experiment has status 'running' but no corresponding worktree exists (check with 'git worktree list | grep nerd-'), warn: 'Nerd: {N} experiments marked running but worktrees missing (possible crashed session). Run /nerd to recover.' If no backlog file exists, say nothing.

Check if the file just written/edited contains new hardcoded numeric thresholds or tunable parameters. Skip files under ~/.claude/plugins/nerd/dag/ — these are research metadata, not codebase parameters. Also skip: UI styling, math constants, protocol values, test fixtures. Look for: float comparisons (>= 0.85), const/DEFAULT_ declarations with numeric values that control behavior, hardcoded timeouts/limits/weights. Before appending, check if .claude/nerd.local.md already has a proposed entry for this exact file:line:value — if so, skip to avoid duplicates. If a strong candidate is found, append it to .claude/nerd.local.md backlog as a 'proposed' entry with id, title, parameter name, file path, line number, and current value. Keep the entry minimal — one line of YAML per finding.
